items.question: Where is the Eiffel Tower located?
items.expected_answer: Paris, France

items.question: Who created Python programming language?
items.expected_answer: Guido van Rossum

items.question: What is machine learning?
items.expected_answer: A subset of AI that enables systems to learn from data

items.question: What is the speed of light?
items.expected_answer: Approximately 299,792,458 meters per second

items.question: What is DNA?
items.expected_answer: Deoxyribonucleic acid that carries genetic instructions

items.question: When was the Eiffel Tower built?
What is photosynthesis?
items.expected_answer: Process by which plants use sunlight, water, and CO2 to produce oxygen and energy

items.question: When did the French Revolution begin?
What is a GPT model?
items.expected_answer: A large language model trained on vast text corpora

items.expected_answer: Attention Is All You Need by Vaswani et al., 2017

items.question: How long is the Great Wall of China?
items.expected_answer: Over 13,000 miles

items.question: In what year was Python created?
Who built the Eiffel Tower?
items.expected_answer: Gustave Eiffel

items.question: What happened after the French Revolution?
items.expected_answer: Rise of Napoleon Bonaparte

items.question: Is Python a compiled or interpreted language?
items.expected_answer: Interpreted

items.question: What do plants produce during photosynthesis?
items.expected_answer: Oxygen and energy

items.question: What is AI?
items.expected_answer: Artificial intelligence — a field enabling machines to simulate intelligence

items.question: What inputs do plants need for photosynthesis?
items.expected_answer: Sunlight, water, and CO2

items.question: What does DNA stand for?
items.expected_answer: Deoxyribonucleic acid

items.question: What is the unit for the speed of light?
items.expected_answer: Meters per second

items.question: What country is Paris in?
items.expected_answer: France

items.question: What kind of model is GPT?
items.expected_answer: A generative pre-trained transformer model

items.question: What does NLI stand for in machine learning?
items.expected_answer: Natural Language Inference

items.question: How many miles long is the Great Wall?
items.expected_answer: Over 13,000 miles

items.question: What is the capital of France?
items.expected_answer: Paris

items.question: Who wrote the Attention Is All You Need paper?
items.expected_answer: Vaswani et al.

items.question: What is hallucination in LLMs?
items.expected_answer: When a model generates content not grounded in its sources or facts

items.question: What does RAG stand for?
items.expected_answer: Retrieval-Augmented Generation

items.question: What is cosine similarity used for?
items.expected_answer: Measuring similarity between two vectors

items.question: What is a transformer in deep learning?
items.expected_answer: An attention-based neural network architecture for sequence modeling

items.question: What is fine-tuning in machine learning?
items.expected_answer: Adapting a pre-trained model to a specific task with additional training

items.question: What is a vector database?
items.expected_answer: A database optimized for storing and querying high-dimensional vector embeddings

items.question: What is prompt engineering?
items.expected_answer: The practice of crafting inputs to guide LLM outputs

items.question: What is p95 latency?
items.expected_answer: The response time below which 95% of requests fall

items.question: What is a golden dataset?
items.expected_answer: A curated set of labeled examples used to evaluate system quality

items.question: What does MLOps stand for?
items.expected_answer: Machine Learning Operations

items.question: What is CI/CD?
items.expected_answer: Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment

items.question: What is a token in NLP?
items.expected_answer: A unit of text such as a word or subword used by language models

items.question: What is embedding in machine learning?
items.expected_answer: A dense vector representation of data in a continuous space

items.question: What is overfitting?
items.expected_answer: When a model performs well on training data but poorly on unseen data

items.question: What is the capital of Germany?
items.expected_answer: Berlin

items.question: What is gradient descent?
items.expected_answer: An optimization algorithm that minimizes loss by iteratively adjusting model weights

items.question: What is cross-entropy loss?
items.expected_answer: A loss function measuring the difference between predicted and true probability distributions

items.question: What is batch normalization?
items.expected_answer: A technique to normalize layer inputs during training to stabilize learning

items.question: What is the attention mechanism?
items.expected_answer: A mechanism allowing models to focus on relevant parts of the input sequence

items.question: What is zero-shot learning?
items.expected_answer: The ability of a model to handle tasks it was not explicitly trained on

items.question: What is few-shot prompting?
items.expected_answer: Providing a few examples in the prompt to guide the model's response

items.question: What is temperature in LLM sampling?
items.expected_answer: A parameter controlling the randomness of token selection; higher = more random

items.question: What is RLHF?
items.expected_answer: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback — a technique to align LLMs with human preferences

items.question: What is a confusion matrix?
items.expected_answer: A table showing actual vs predicted classifications for a model

items.question: What is precision in ML evaluation?
items.expected_answer: Fraction of positive predictions that are actually correct

items.question: What is recall in ML evaluation?
items.expected_answer: Fraction of actual positives correctly identified by the model

items.question: What is the F1 score?
items.expected_answer: The harmonic mean of precision and recall

items.question: What is AUC-ROC?
items.expected_answer: Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic curve — measures classifier performance

items.question: What is BLEU score?
items.expected_answer: A metric for evaluating machine translation by comparing n-gram overlap with reference

items.question: What is semantic search?
items.expected_answer: Search that understands meaning rather than exact keyword matching

items.question: What is a knowledge base in RAG?
items.expected_answer: A collection of documents the retriever searches to provide context

items.question: What is chunking in RAG pipelines?
items.expected_answer: Splitting documents into smaller pieces for efficient retrieval

items.question: What is a system prompt?
items.expected_answer: Instructions given to the LLM before the user message to set behavior and context

items.question: What is context window in LLMs?
items.expected_answer: The maximum number of tokens an LLM can process in a single call

items.question: What is tokenization?
items.expected_answer: The process of splitting text into tokens before feeding it to a language model

items.question: What is a language model?
items.expected_answer: A model trained to predict or generate text based on learned patterns

items.question: What is model quantization?
items.expected_answer: Reducing model precision (e.g., from float32 to int8) to decrease size and speed up inference

items.question: What does GPU stand for?
items.expected_answer: Graphics Processing Unit

items.question: What is inference in machine learning?
items.expected_answer: Using a trained model to generate predictions on new data

items.question: Who founded OpenAI?
items.expected_answer: OpenAI was founded by Sam Altman, Elon Musk, and others in 2015

items.question: What is the Turing Test?
items.expected_answer: A test of a machine's ability to exhibit intelligent behavior indistinguishable from a human

items.question: What is a neural network?
items.expected_answer: A computational model inspired by the brain, composed of layers of interconnected nodes

items.question: What is backpropagation?
items.expected_answer: An algorithm for training neural networks by computing gradients of the loss function

items.question: What is dropout in neural networks?
items.expected_answer: A regularization technique that randomly deactivates neurons during training to prevent overfitting

items.question: What city hosted the 2024 Summer Olympics?
items.expected_answer: Paris, France

items.question: What is a hyperparameter?
items.expected_answer: A parameter set before training that controls the learning process (e.g., learning rate)

items.question: What is transfer learning?
items.expected_answer: Using a pre-trained model as a starting point for a new task

items.question: What is the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?
items.expected_answer: Supervised uses labeled data; unsupervised finds patterns in unlabeled data

items.question: What is a convolutional neural network?
items.expected_answer: A neural network designed for processing grid-like data such as images

items.question: What is data augmentation?
items.expected_answer: Artificially expanding training data through transformations like rotation or flipping

items.question: What is a recurrent neural network?
items.expected_answer: A neural network designed for sequential data with feedback connections

items.question: What is the role of an optimizer in training?
items.expected_answer: To update model weights by minimizing the loss function

items.question: What is BERT?
items.expected_answer: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers — a language model by Google

items.question: What is the capital of Japan?
items.expected_answer: Tokyo

items.question: What is a loss function?
items.expected_answer: A function measuring how far model predictions are from true values

items.question: What is the difference between precision and recall?
items.expected_answer: Precision measures correct positive predictions; recall measures how many actual positives were found

items.question: What is a benchmark in AI?
items.expected_answer: A standardized test to compare model performance across systems

items.question: What is named entity recognition?
items.expected_answer: NLP task of identifying and classifying entities like names and places in text

items.question: What is sentiment analysis?
items.expected_answer: Determining the emotional tone or opinion expressed in text

items.question: What is question answering in NLP?
items.expected_answer: The task of automatically answering questions posed in natural language

items.question: What is the transformer architecture used for and who introduced it?
items.expected_answer: The transformer is used for sequence modeling and was introduced by Vaswani et al. in 2017

items.question: Which language was created before Python — Java or Python?
items.expected_answer: Python was created in 1991; Java in 1995, so Python came first

items.question: How does photosynthesis relate to animal life?
items.expected_answer: Plants produce oxygen via photosynthesis which animals need to breathe

items.question: What is the relationship between DNA and living organisms?
items.expected_answer: DNA carries genetic instructions for the development of all living organisms

items.question: Was the Eiffel Tower built before or after the French Revolution?
items.expected_answer: After — French Revolution was 1789, Eiffel Tower was built in 1889

items.question: What is the most important concept in AI?
items.expected_answer: This is subjective; key concepts include neural networks, training, and generalization

items.question: What high-level language was Guido van Rossum known for creating in 1991?
items.expected_answer: Python

items.question: Which component of a cell carries genetic instructions?
items.expected_answer: DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid)

items.question: What does the Transformer paper title suggest about its core mechanism?
items.expected_answer: That attention is the only mechanism needed — 'Attention Is All You Need'

items.question: In which country was Napoleon Bonaparte's rise triggered?
items.expected_answer: France, following the French Revolution

items.question: What makes a language model generative?
items.expected_answer: It can produce new text by predicting the next token based on learned patterns
